name: iced
description: Elm-inspired, pure-Rust, wgpu renderer, active and popular

first-release.comment: First crates.io publish was version 0.0.0 on May 29, 2019.

maintenance-status.comment: 0.14 released Dec 2025 after a long development cycle; master branch shows ongoing commits toward 0.15.

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (currently 0.14) but production-proven (COSMIC, Halloy, Liana). API still evolves between minor releases.

corporate-backing.value: Community-driven. Historically sponsored by the Cryptowatch team at Kraken.com; System76 actively contributes via its COSMIC desktop fork.
corporate-backing.comment: Creator Héctor Ramón describes iced as his personal project per the FAQ.

rendering-mode.comment: View function produces a widget tree that iced reconciles; 0.14 added reactive rendering (on-demand redraws) by default.

reactivity-model.comment: Classic Elm architecture: Message enum + update/view functions.

thread-model.comment: UI runs on the main thread; async tasks via Command/futures can run on a thread pool, Tokio, or smol.

renderer.comment: iced_wgpu (Vulkan/Metal/DX12/GL via wgpu) is the default; iced_tiny_skia provides a software fallback. WebGL feature enables browser targets.

vector-rendering.comment: Canvas widget and svg feature flag provide vector drawing primitives.

custom-shaders.comment: custom_shader example demonstrates injecting a user-defined wgpu render pipeline.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: custom_shader and integration examples embed custom wgpu/3D content inside the iced UI.

linux-wayland.comment: Default features include `wayland`; used natively by the COSMIC desktop environment.

ios.comment: docs.rs lists only Windows, Linux, and macOS as target platforms. No official iOS support.

android.comment: docs.rs lists only Windows, Linux, and macOS as target platforms. No official Android support.

web-wasm.comment: Web target via the `webgl` feature; mentioned as cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux, and the Web) in the README.

authoring-style.comment: Views are built with Rust functions and builder methods; convenience macros (row!, column!) exist but are thin sugar over the builder API.

hot-reload.comment: 0.14 introduced a `hot` feature flag and associated tooling for reloading UI code during development.

layout-system.comment: Row/column containers with Length::Fill/Shrink/Fixed sizing behave like a simplified flexbox.

styling-approach.comment: Built-in Theme variants (Light, Dark, Nord, etc.) plus per-widget style functions and inline props.

theme-switching.comment: Runtime theme changes are supported; 0.14 added automatic reaction to the system light/dark theme.

accessibility.comment: AccessKit integration is in progress (driven by System76 for COSMIC); issue #552 remains open and full a11y is not landed in mainline as of 0.14.

internationalization.comment: 0.14 added IME (input method editor) support for non-Latin input, but iced provides no built-in localization framework — users wire up a crate like fluent or rust-i18n themselves.

widget-breadth.comment: Common widgets plus 0.14 additions: table, grid, markdown, float, pin, QR code, canvas, svg. Missing: tree view, native menus, first-party charts.

data-grid-table.comment: Table (and grid) widget added in 0.14; virtualization behavior not independently verified.

rich-text-markdown.comment: First-class markdown widget (behind `markdown` feature) landed in 0.14, with customizable rendering and image support.

tree-outline.comment: No built-in tree/outline widget; users rely on nested column+button patterns or community crates.

native-menus.comment: No native menu bar or context menu support; iced draws its own UI entirely.

file-dialogs.comment: No built-in file dialogs; apps integrate the external `rfd` crate.

charts-plotting.comment: No first-party chart widget; `plotters-iced` is the community option.

custom-widgets.comment: Documented `advanced` module exposes the Widget trait; examples (custom_widget, custom_quad, loupe) show the pattern with moderate boilerplate.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Feature flags `tokio`, `smol`, and default `thread-pool` select the async executor.

system-tray.comment: No built-in system tray; requires external crate (e.g., tray-icon).

notifications.comment: No built-in OS notifications; requires external crate (e.g., notify-rust).

drag-and-drop.comment: No built-in OS-level drag-and-drop; the community `iced_drop` crate adds in-app drag-and-drop.

multi-window.comment: `window` module and `multi_window` example demonstrate multiple top-level windows; 0.14 also adds a daemon mode.

binary-size.comment: Not independently measured here; wgpu + image pipeline typically produce stripped binaries in the 10-30 MB range. Verify for your build.

startup-time.comment: Hacker News discussion reports cold start around a few hundred ms to ~2s depending on hardware and wgpu backend init. Not independently measured.

memory-footprint.comment: Hacker News thread reports ~70 MB idle for a minimal app (within the 20-80 MB "low" bucket). Not independently measured.

idle-redraw.comment: 0.14 enables reactive rendering by default so the window only redraws when state changes.

dependency-count.comment: The 9 direct iced_* sub-crates plus wgpu/image/winit pull in well over 200 transitive crates in a typical build. Not independently measured with cargo tree.

documentation-quality.comment: Official book + FAQ + comprehensive API docs + 56 examples. Book was rewritten around 0.13 and tracks current APIs; some community material lags.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic Rust with strong type safety, builder methods, and typed Messages. Some verbosity (explicit Message enums, view/update split) is inherent to Elm.

interactive-playground.comment: No official online playground; demos require local compilation. The tour example is the canonical showcase.

ide-tooling.comment: No iced-specific LSP/inspector/formatter beyond what rust-analyzer provides for any Rust project.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: 0.14 introduced the `comet` debugger with time-travel and devtools foundations (behind the `time-travel` / `debug` features).

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level `iced` crate as of 2026-04-24 (~109k/month).

notable-production-users.value: COSMIC desktop environment (System76 / Pop!_OS), Halloy (IRC client), Liana (Bitcoin wallet), Sniffnet (network monitor), Veloren launcher, ESLauncher2.

community-size.comment: Active Discord, dedicated Discourse forum, ~30k GitHub stars, regular external contributors.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Notable crates: iced_aw (widget extensions), iced_audio, iced_video_player, iced_drop, iced_dialog, plotters-iced, cosmic-text, cosmic-time, nih-plug (audio plugins), bevy_iced.
